title: HoliLabs Telemedicine Video Subsystem — Architecture & Security
description: WebRTC provider abstraction, TURN/STUN infrastructure for LATAM, recording pipeline, bandwidth adaptation, and HIPAA compliance.

providerAbstraction.description: Allow switching between Daily.co, Twilio Video, and custom TURN infrastructure without code changes.

recordingPipeline.description: Server-side recording composition and storage pipeline (opt-in per encounter)
flow: 2. Server-side compositor combines streams (doctor + patient + screen share if enabled)
3. Encoded output (H.264 + AAC) → S3 (holilabs-protected region, encrypted at rest)
4. Post-processing: transcription via AWS Transcribe Medical, speaker diarization
5. Indexed in DynamoDB for compliance audit, retention = 5 years per LGPD

storage.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
storage.keyManagement: AWS KMS + customer-managed keys per org

retention.deletion: automated via S3 Lifecycle after expiry, audit logged

transcription.output: VTT + SRT with timestamps + medical term confidence scores
transcription.pii_handling: automatic redaction of patient names, dates of birth

bandwidthAdaptation.description: Graceful degradation based on network conditions
bandwidthAdaptation.adaptation_algorithm: REMB (Receiver Estimated Maximum Bitrate) from WebRTC, client-side congestion control
bandwidthAdaptation.fallback_media: Audio-only if video bitrate < 100 kbps for > 30s

cyrus_invariants.sessionTokenValidation: All room join attempts require server-validated JWT with orgId, userId, encounterId, TTL 24h
cyrus_invariants.recordingConsent: Recording cannot start without encounter.recordingConsent === true AND user JWT contains 'record' claim
cyrus_invariants.organizationScoping: All video sessions scoped to organizationId; cross-tenant join attempts rejected
cyrus_invariants.bypassMechanism: Internal health checks bypass auth via X-Health-Check-Secret (HMAC-SHA256, rotated daily)

e2eEncryption.description: Optional Dtls-SRTP E2E encryption (ZRTP-like) for ultra-sensitive encounters
e2eEncryption.note: Disabled recording while E2E active (server cannot access unencrypted streams)
e2eEncryption.use_case: Psychiatry/mental health encounters where recording is not permitted

hipaaCompliance.baas: Daily.co + Twilio both have HIPAA BAAs signed
hipaaCompliance.encryption: TLS 1.3 for all media (UDP via DTLS)
hipaaCompliance.storage: Recordings on S3 with KMS encryption + MFA delete enabled
hipaaCompliance.audit: All access logged to CloudTrail + custom audit table in RDS

publicHealthDeclaration.note: Brazilian health data is considered 'sensitive personal data' under LGPD. AI Scribe / transcription processing requires explicit Art. 7 consent.
publicHealthDeclaration.sopsCompliance: All recording metadata included in SOAP note structured data (ICD-10, SNOMED, LOINC codes)

elenaiInvariants.aiScribeDuringVideo: If encounter.aiScribeEnabled === true, display SaMD disclaimer in waiting room: 'AI Scribe is enabled. This session may be recorded for transcription.'
elenaiInvariants.clinicalLogging: All video session start/stop events logged via /api/clinical/events with userId, encounterId, duration, outcome (ended_normal / ended_early / error)
elenaiInvariants.copilotContext: Copilot receives transcript chunks (100ms latency) for real-time suggestion of diagnoses, medication dosage, ICD-10 codes
elenaiInvariants.disclaimer: Copilot suggestions tagged with ⚠️ 'Clinical Decision Support — not a diagnosis' in UI

metrics: offer_latency (ms) — time from join attempt to CONNECTED state
recording_lag (sec) — delay of recorded frames vs live

perEncounter.recordingStorageQuota: 500 MB per month per org (soft limit, P3 alert at 80%)

deploymentSteps: 1. Set DAILY_API_TOKEN or TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN in .env (provider selection in config.ts)
2. Reserve custom TURN servers on AWS EC2 (coturn) in br-sp-1, co-bo-1 regions
4. Upload recordings bucket policy to S3 (encryption, versioning, MFA delete)
5. Configure CloudTrail logging for S3 bucket access
6. Set up Twilio/Daily webhooks for session events (room_finish, participant_left, recording_ready)
7. Test end-to-end: waiting room → preview → join → peer connection → record → S3 upload
8. Lighthouse CI check: Core Web Vitals during video call (should remain high quality)
